items.title: The immersive Indigenous language class where English is left at the door

children.content: After learning from Native American Yuchi families, Pertame elders and young mothers in Alice Springs are raising babies in a total immersion Language Nest and hope to extend the model into primary school.

image.altText: A woman plays blocks with a child.

items.title: NT's deputy opposition leader dumped for new recruit

children.content: The Northern Territory Labor party has replaced its deputy opposition leader with a newcomer to parliament, in a move they say will win back the trust and confidence of the people of Darwin.

image.altText: A man wearing a red shirt standing in a shed, looking directly down the camera.

items.title: NT government extends town camp alcohol bans

children.content: The NT government is extending alcohol bans in town camps beyond 2027, in a move welcomed by the health sector as a win for community safety.

gallery.altText: A row of empty liquor bottles lined up on a kitchen bench, the adjoining walls are covered in marks and dents.

items.title: In pictures: Alice Springs celebrates 30 years of unique craft festival

children.content: As the Alice Springs Beanie Festival celebrates its 30th and final year, we're exploring the weird and wonderful handmade creations — and the characters working hard behind the scenes — that have made the annual event so special.

image.altText: A man smiling at the camera, on his head is a handmade beanie featuring a knitted monkey holding a banana.

items.title: Call for youth foyer to help tackle homelessness in the NT

children.content: Youth homelessness rates remain stubbornly high in Central Australia and advocates are renewing calls for a transitional housing model for young people to be established to tackle the issue.

image.altText: a pile of pillows on a sandy ground with bushland in the background

items.title: NT government reveals scale of bungled smart meter rollout

children.content: The Northern Territory's main energy retailer, Jacana, reveals about 5,000 customers — thousands more than previously thought — have been caught up in a smart meter bungle leading to long delays in their billing.

image.altText: A smart meter

items.title: Couple forced to live on verandah for three months awaiting flood repairs

children.content: Katherine couple Nicki and Luke Asling have been living on their back verandah for the past three months, after their home was flooded to the ceiling in March. Local renovators say it will take months for all the repairs in town to be completed.

image.altText: A woman sits on a bed outdoors, patting a dog.

items.title: Why this Paraguay star was red-carded for covering his mouth

children.content: Paraguay's Miguel Almirón fell victim to new laws put in place at this World Cup to counter discriminatory behaviour. So what are the new laws in place?

articles.title: Cristian Volpato shows why the Socceroos wanted him

children.content: Cristian Volpato only made a 30-minute cameo for the Socceroos in their 2-0 World Cup defeat against the USA, but in doing so showed his quality and desire to excel for Australia.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato runs with the ball

articles.title: Quick hits: NFL flavour at the World Cup and Osito the dog charms fans

children.content: Scotland feels hard done by in their defeat against Morocco and a former NFL legend is on hand to photograph as the son of another of the NFL alumni cohort scores for his country. Here's the quick hits from the FIFA World Cup.

articles.title: Socceroos receive World Cup wake-up call and have a week to find answers

items.title: 'Everything's just so wrong': Inside the country town that turned to One Nation

items.title: What does it mean for a nation to be a 'monoculture'?

children.content: Pauline Hanson has called for Australia to reject multiculturalism and be "monocultural". So what does it involve and are there any nations overseas that show what it might look like in Australia?
